disease.name: Phenytoin Therapeutic Drug Monitoring
disease.note: Phenytoin has non-linear (Michaelis-Menten) pharmacokinetics - small dose changes cause large serum level changes. Free (unbound) level monitoring is critical in hypoalbuminemia and uremia. Corrected total phenytoin = measured total / (0.2 x albumin + 0.1).

severityAssessment.severityLabel: If level >30 or neurotoxicity -> TOXIC; if <10 with seizures -> SUBTHERAPEUTIC; if 10-20 -> THERAPEUTIC

subtypes.name: Subtherapeutic Levels (<10 mcg/mL)
subtypes.management: Assess adherence. Check for interacting drugs. Correct total for albumin. Increase dose by 50-100mg/day increments. Recheck level in 5-7 days (1-2 weeks for steady state). Consider switch to alternative if poor compliance.

subtypes.name: Phenytoin Toxicity (>20 mcg/mL)
subtypes.management: Hold phenytoin until level therapeutic. Check free level if hypoalbuminemic. Assess for nystagmus, ataxia, mental status. If severe: consider fosphenytoin discontinuation, benzodiazepines for seizures, monitor airway.

subtypes.name: Phenytoin Hypersensitivity (DRESS/SJS)
subtypes.management: Immediately discontinue phenytoin. Switch to alternative antiepileptic (levetiracetam, valproate). Systemic corticosteroids for severe DRESS. IVIG for SJS/TEN. HLA-B*1502 screening in Asian patients before starting.

bedside.name: Neurological exam for nystagmus, ataxia
bedside.indication: all patients on phenytoin with symptoms

laboratory.name: Total phenytoin (trough level, steady state)
laboratory.indication: routine monitoring, toxicity, or subtherapeutic concern

laboratory.name: Free phenytoin level
laboratory.indication: hypoalbuminemia, uremia, pregnancy, or total level discrepancy with clinical status

management.protocolReference: IUPHAR/BPS Guide to TDM, Epilepsy Foundation Guidelines

pathways.criteria: total level <10 mcg/mL, corrected level <10, breakthrough seizures
pathways.treatment: Increase dose by 25-50mg/day increments. Monitor free level if indicated. Recheck in 5-7 days. Consider loading dose if urgent (fosphenytoin IV/IM).

pathways.criteria: total level 10-20 mcg/mL (corrected if low albumin), seizure-free, no toxicity
pathways.treatment: Continue current dose. Schedule routine level q6-12 months. Monitor when interacting drugs added/stopped. Check if clinical status changes.

pathways.criteria: total level >20-30 mcg/mL, or any signs of neurotoxicity
pathways.treatment: Hold next 1-2 doses. Recheck level in 2-3 days. Restart at lower dose when level 10-15. If severe toxicity (level >40, coma, seizures): ICU, consider charcoal hemoperfusion, seizure management.
